What is the difference between common law and civil law?

Commonlaw societies were formed before civil code was written. So, common law has a earlier dating than civil code. Civil law includes both common law and personalized codes. When we talk about civil law we are talking about the legal obligations of a person to another party. In contrast, common law involves the personal behavior of a person which leads to consequences for not following through on those behaviors. An example of common law is "the preemption doctrine." This doctrine states thata person may not enforce a right under s. 1 of the constitution unless it has been specifically granted in writing. The grace period given to enforce these rights frequently ends sooner than anticipatedespecially if there is a summary judgement executed by the government.
Personalized codes were written after civil code. Compared to common law, civil law has more provisions and definitions that can be used to enforced rights. It also mentions specific cases that define the scope of rights under sections 5-7. Common law only mentions general principles around how to protect rights; specific definitions of rights exist in civil law texts. For example, section 6 of the French Code de Personalité provides: "Les	dieux, dont l'habillage est dans le respect requis par la loi, de toute violence, de全部alaible à leur puce, pour protéger les intérêts de leurs descendants et héritiers, qui sont reçus de manière universelle, sans prévention." (The heirs, from whose land is required by law to be withdrawn, shall, without prejudice, claim any priority which they may have with reference to the distribution of property among不止一个 descendant generation.) 
In conclusion, civil law dates back to before the Constitution was written while common law refers to the laws that relate to civil conduct.

As someone who enjoys hard science fiction, I was disappointed in how similar (and at times, identical) several of the plot points were from one book to another. While this may have been intended as repetition to build momentum and create anticipation, it really didn't maintain any underlying themes or developments throughout the series. Consequently, I found myself losing track of what was going on more quickly than I would like have done had I not already finished the series. 

Expanse offers little original character development over its stated purpose, beyond the main three characters, James Mars, Alex O'Brien, and Janeway. At multiple points, I found myself wondering if I had previously read any of these characters in another work, particularly Alex O'Brien. This lack of new character development should have set my jaw: surely there must be something unique about the way Martian Chronicles examines the human condition? Was I missing something critical here? 

Yes, there are a few redeeming factors. The worldsbuilding and characterization of the various planets is fascinating, and certainly rivals that of George Orwell's_1984_. The solar system politics, intermixed with themes of artificial intelligence versus humanity, religion versus science, and civilization vs. apocalypse, are equally interesting. And no exoplanets title sequence could ever compete with the breathtaking end sequences of "The End" and "The Return."

### Instruction:
What are the key benefits of Delta Live Tables?

### Response:
generated_text:
Delta Live Tables (DLT) is a declarative managed framework for building reliable, maintainable, and testable data pipelines. DLT offers the following capabilities:
-- Tables
-- Data Sources
-- Filters & Aggregates
-- Union: filters and aggregates on the same table
-- Intersection: only include rows where two or more tables have matching filter expressions
-- Difference: only include rows where one or more tables match a specific pattern
-- All: include all the tables in the default location
-- Clone: create a copy of current table structures and functionalities for testing and development
-- Update：Add new columns to an existing table

See https://strapi.io/docs/datalab/deep-dive/#table-contents to learn about TableStructure and https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SQL_query_to_learnAbout SQL queries.

For decision support systems using rules based on human input, the inputs and outputs comply with standard criteria such as cost, efficiency, and usefulness. In contrast, for automated systems using metrics-based objectives, the development of the AI system itself determines the level of quality. Nevertheless, despite the use of different methods, all AI systems produced by humans will ultimately pass through evaluation by humans. Thus, the process of AI governance involves developing mechanisms that ensure that final users gain reasonable outcomes even if those outcomes are difficult to quantify directly. Since autonomous agents will eventually replace humans in rule-making tasks, it is important that these agents also promote fairness and respect human rights in decisions that affect human lives.

Once you're in an onsen village, it's quite common to be surrounded by stunning natural beauty. Walking around town isn't always possible, but if you see something interesting, say your name, and raise your hand, locals will likely stop and talk to you. English is widely spoken in most regions, so don't worry too much about making basic gestures!

### Instruction:
What are the disadvantages of Linux?

### Response:
generated_text:
The major disadvantage ofLinux is that it is an open source software which means that it is freely available for anyone to copy, change and distribute as they wish. This has led to a huge variety of distributions available today. However, because of the licenses of various components which it depends on, some consider it risky development environment for hardware and software applications.
Secondly, unlike Windows, Linux does not have an installer default installation. It must be installed by hand for each user who needs to use it. This can be difficult for users who are inexperienced with computer maintenance, and creates more complexity for companies whose IT teams may not have experience with Unix-like systems.
Thirdly, although Linux is widely used in servers and embedded systems, it is notoriously hard to develop applications for. This is because the system requires a separate package for almost every application one might want to develop, and developers need to download separate code from sources across all the projects they might want to integrate. This greatly limits the ability to develop multi-platform apps quickly.

How can I better learn a new language?

### Response:

Language learning requires continuous and regular practice and many hours of effort. However there are tried and true techniques for improving your grammar and fluency quickly:

1) Immerse yourself in the foreign language by reading magazine articles, books, and newspapers.  Also watch movies, television shows, or online videos in the target language, preferably with subtitles in the foreign language.  In addition, you can listen to music and podcasts in the language that you are studying.  The goal is to find a variety of content on diverse topics that interest you and to develop both listening and reading skills. 

2) Find a meet-up group or online conversation partner that is willing to speak with you on a variety of topics in the foreign language you are studying.  Because there are many people who are interested in learning your language, you should be able to find someone who can help you with their's. 

3) Use computer Apps like Duolingo to help you memorize vocabulary.  Apps like Duolingo or Babbel help you learn vocabulary through constant repetition of important words.  Through regular, daily practice, you will find it easier to remember words when you are conversing with others.    

4) Learn how to read and write in the foreign language you are studying.  By reading and writing in this language, you will improve your grammatical skills as well as your ability to express your thoughts. Gardening is the practice of growing and cultivating plants as part of horticulture. In gardens, ornamental plants are often grown for their flowers, foliage, or overall appearance; useful plants, such as root vegetables, leaf vegetables, fruits, and herbs, are grown for consumption, for use as dyes, or for medicinal or cosmetic use.

Gardening ranges in scale from fruit orchards, to long boulevard plantings with one or more different types of shrubs, trees, and herbaceous plants, to residential back gardens including lawns and foundation plantings, all the way to container gardens grown inside or outside. Gardening may be very specialized, with only one type of plant grown, or involve a variety of plants in mixed plantings. It involves an active participation in the growing of plants, and tends to be labor-intensive, which differentiates it from farming or forestry.

The five whys were initially developed to understand why new product features or manufacturing techniques were needed, and was not developed for root cause analysis.

### Instruction:
What are some languages that feature hygienic macros?

### Input:
Hygienic macros are macros whose expansion is guaranteed not to cause the accidental capture of identifiers. They are a feature of programming languages such as Scheme,[1] Dylan,[2] Rust, Nim, and Julia. The general problem of accidental capture was well known within the Lisp community prior to the introduction of hygienic macros. Macro writers would use language features that would generate unique identifiers (e.g., gensym) or use obfuscated identifiers in order to avoid the problem. Hygienic macros are a programmatic solution to the capture problem that is integrated into the macro expander itself. The term "hygiene" was coined in Kohlbecker et al.'s 1986 paper that introduced hygienic macro expansion, inspired by the terminology used in mathematics.[3]

### Response:
generated_text: Macros featuring hygénic expansions include Scheme, Dylan, Rust, Nim, and Julia.

The mutineers put then-Lieutenant Bligh and eighteen other crew into a tiny, open boat and set them adrift in the middle of the South Pacific ocean.  Bligh and his men had just a small amount of food and water.  Bligh used his memory of navigation charts to plan a course westward to the Dutch colony of Timor.  

They initially tried stopping at an island to gather supplies.  The island was inhabited, however, and they were attacked.  One crew member was killed and the rest barely escaped.  From then on they dared not stop at any island until reaching the Great Barrier Reef of Australia (then called New Holland by the Europeans).

In a remarkable achievement of seamanship, Bligh and his men sailed and rowed their open boat for 41 days over a distance of 3618 miles, in sometimes harsh weather conditions.  Food and drink had to be strictly rationed, and the men suffered greatly from hunger and thirst.  Except for the death of the crewman at the hands of the islanders, all the remaining crew reached Timor alive.  Sadly, some men died of disease contracted at the colony town of Batavia or on the final return voyage to Europe.  Of the original nineteen men set adrift by the Bounty Hunters, twelve returned alive to England.

Bligh continued his career in the British Navy.  He was assigned a new ship and once again sailed to Tahiti take breadfruit to British colonies in the Caribbean.  This time, the mission was completed successfully.  Bligh commanded additional ships, including fighting in several major naval battles.  In 1805, Bligh was appointed Governor of New South Wales.  He ended his British Naval career as a Vice Admiral.  William Bligh died in London on Dec 12, 1817.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What is the difference between American Football and European Football?

### Response:
generated_text: American football and european football are two different sports. American football, also known as gridiron, was first played in 1869 in the United States and mostly known in the United States and Canada. It rivals two teams on a rectangular field. Both teams have an offence as well as a defence formation. The first offence and defence of the match is decided by coin tossed where the winner decide for the first half of the match while the opposite match starts at the half-time mark. The offence team tries to take the oval-shaped ball to the other side of the field by passing or running it at each play. The defence team blocks the attackants so they can gain possession of the ball themselves. Then, both teams swap positions. The new vice-president of offense goes from the defense to the offense and vice versa. When there is no scoring action, then the team with the most points wins. Points are earned by grounding the ball over the line before your opposition does the same. If both sides make contact with the ground at the same time, the ball is considered as having been touched by either team simultaneously. A unique aspect of American football is that when a player catches the ball, their hands must be not shorter than the legs of the person throwing the ball to them! Additionally, any form of disengagement from foot (contacting the ground) will result in a penalty box clock being used during the return trip to the house point. Lastly, offense takes the ball out into space and attempts to shoot it using four players opposed to just one. Whenever an offensive player touches the ball out into space, another offensive player gets the opportunity to catch the ball directly from that position. This mechanism is called Man Down. The winning team earns three points if they have the initiative to advance the ball out into space and shoot it, instead of only two points if they don't. European football, also know as soccer, is a match between two teams of eleven players on a rectangular field, known as pitch. Players primarily use their feet to propel a sphere-shaped ball across the field into the rectangular shaped goal guarded by a goalkeeper. The goalkeeper is the only player of each team that can use his hands and arms on the ball within specific areas of the field close to their goals. If a player touches the ball out or out-of-bounds before the opposing team's goalkeeper can grasp it, that turn is counted as a turnover. Although in reverse, once inside the enemy territory, a player may receive a yellow card which would lead to a penalty kick

What’s a recipe for chimichurri?

Chimichurri is a traditional Argentine sauce whose base ingredients include parsley, garlic and oil. The local term for chimichurri is piccata. 

Ingredients
- 1 tablespoon olive oil 
- 2 tablespoons dried parsley 
- 3 tablespoons diced garlic 
- 1/4 cup red onion 
- 1 (10-ounce) can tomato sauce 
- 1 large clove garlic, minced 
- 1/2 ounce grated Parmesan cheese 
- Salt 
- Pepper

Instructions


Process

Add the oil to a small pot. Add the garlic and stir. Add the onion and cook on medium heat until soft. Stir in the Tomato paste. Add the tomato juice, stirring quickly. Add theParmesan cheese, salt and pepper. Cook on low to medium heat for several hours until the mixture reduces and coats the pan. If it's getting too dry, add a little bit of water or tomato puree. Let it reduce some more then use the chimney method to separate the solids from the liquid. Discard the hard sides of the peppers. Chop up the cooked onions and return to the pot. Add additional water if needed to bring the mixture to a gentle boil. Once boiled, remove from the heat. Cover and let cool down fully before storing in the fridge overnight. Alternatively, you can also blend the ingredients together in a blender. Then pour them through a strainer to catch the seeds and herbs. Finally, mix them with vegan butter and pulse once. Enjoy over warm days.

### Instruction:
What is rheological weldability?

### Input:
Rheological weldability (RW) of thermoplastics considers the materials flow characteristics in determining the weldability of the given material.[1] The process of welding thermal plastics requires three general steps, first is surface preparation. The second step is the application of heat and pressure to create intimate contact between the components being joined and initiate inter-molecular diffusion across the joint and the third step is cooling.[2] RW can be used to determine the effectiveness of the second step of the process for given materials.

### Response:
generated_text: Rheological Weldability: It is a property that measures how well a substance will solidify under certain conditions. Depending on the value, the substance can be said to have high or low melt volatility. When referring to thermoplastics, theelastic properties of these plastics depend on how they interact with heated surfaces. For example, high Rheonomic Index (HR) values cause higher melting points and thus harder plastics to harden under hot terminals. On the other hand, lower HR values cause softer plastics to easily deform under similar stress scenarios.urgentities like automotive industry rely on this-property when selecting suitable joints for their products, ranging from engine blocks to car bodies.

What are the advatages and disadvatages of commuting with bikes comparing to cars?

### Response:
generated_text:
Advantages
- You can ride at any time of the day from about 30 minutes to several hours. The classic bike commute is between 30 minutes and an hour depending on the target location.
- Bikes are friendly to the environment. They do not emit any exhaust fumes or smoke, and they do not contribute to air pollution. Also, unlike cars, they do not generate noise for the surrounding communities.
- Bikes are safe. According to the American Lung Association, riding a bike in the USA for transportation "is healthier than driving a car because the accident rates are lower". Additionally, a lot of the roads were designed specifically with cyclists in mind, such as shared paths and wide shoulders. Bicycling is also safer than driving, especially for children who are still learning drivers by observing others.

Disadvantages
-_bike lanes are rare_ , so you may have to dodge large vehicles when crossing streets. In some cities, this is even necessary if you want to use the public bike system._
- Public bikes are generally owned by municipalities and rented out via libraries or community centers. This makes it hard to find a bike for rent or purchase, unless you want to spend a lot of money on it. 
- Bike commuting is most enjoyable during temperate climates. When it is warm, you might actually enjoy chipping away at your commute instead of grinding through cold, uncomfortable weather. But when it's raining, or if you're visiting places like New York City, San Francisco, Los Angeles, or Sydney, Australia, having access to reliable bicycle infrastructure becomes nearly impossible. In those situations, you might consider an electric bike if you don't mind the weight, which typically ranges from 100 lbs (40 kg) to over 800 lbs (400 kg).

### Instruction:
What caused the Global Financial Crises?

### Input:
The preconditioning for the financial crisis were complex and multi-causal.[5][6][7] Almost two decades prior, the U.S. Congress had passed legislation encouraging financing for affordable housing.[8] However, in 1999, parts of the Glass-Steagall legislation, which had been adopted in 1933, were repealed, permitting financial institutions to commingle their commercial (risk-averse) and proprietary trading (risk-taking) operations.[9] Arguably the largest contributor to the conditions necessary for financial collapse was the rapid development in predatory financial products which targeted low-income, low-information homebuyers who largely belonged to racial minorities.[10] This market development went unattended by regulators and thus caught the U.S. government by surprise.[11]

### Response:
generated_text: The Global Financial Crisis (GFC) was caused by the reckless lending of banks and money clubs across the world. In particular, the growth of subprime mortgage lending in the United States contributed significantly to the GFC. Originating in the United States in 2007, the persistent high interest rate environment and the bursting of the United States hosing bubble drove hundreds of thousands of mortgages over foreclosure. Nations around the world then followed suit and implemented large scale deregulating of credit markets. As a result, nearly every country experienced exponential growth in predatory lending practices targeting low income, low information home buyers. The only regulation keeping these products out of circulation was the limited capacity of most countries to contain excessive risk-taking within their borders. Ultimately, the greed of individuals and companies fueled the tensions rising to devastating levels within the global financial system.

Why is the sky blue?

All of the light in our daytime skies comes from the sun, and our sun produces light in many different colors. To understand this, just think of a prism.  White light enters, and a rainbow of colors exits. Our sun is like that. Its light may seem uniform but it is actually emitting many different colors.

Our sky though is not a mix of blue and red and green because the earth's atmosphere is sandwiched between the sun and us , getting in the way. Atmospheric gases and particles scatter the incoming Colors, bouncing them in all different directions.  The amount of scattering increases as the wavelength of light decreases.  Red light which has a long wavelength is scattered much less than blue light which has a short wavelength. As blue is scattered more, it fills our daytime skies.

As the sun traverses the sky and moves low on the horizon, the light that reaches our eyes must actually travel further through the atmosphere. ### Instruction:
What is the appeal to baby led weaning?

### Response:
generated_text: The weaning process should be done with care so that children do not become reliant on food rather than nurturing and love from their caregivers. The idea behind baby led weaning is that babies are fed by sensing when their gums are touchable. For example, a caregiver can hold out their thumb and the baby will suck on that to sense whether the tongue has been cleaned or not. This method is gentle on the digestive system as the teeth are not yet fully developed and the sucking action helps promote saliva production to clean the teeth. Saliva also contains enzymes that help break down nutrients and make them easier for the body to absorb. Thus, sensing drool comes before feeding. This approach also allows for greater flexibility in weaning times, as there is no physical restriction on what foods are safe for consuming. Weaning toys can be used at different stages, ranging from putting milk into a cup, which is safe for little ones until they turn 1, through soliciting food from a newborn whose only skill is eating from a spoon. At this young age, signs of hunger including increased crying and excitement are key indicators that a child is hungry. When using this method, it is important to make sure that all safety precautions are taken, including keeping hot water or nursery rhymes away from infants and making sure baby is comfortable before starting the weaning process.

The Malibamat'so forms the northern arm of the Katse Dam reservoir, a part of the Lesotho Highlands Water Project. prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What is functional genomics?

### Response:
generated_text:
Functional genomics seeks to describe the function of genes and their influence on cellular processes throughout the organism. While genotype消灭影响其表达的程度而生存, its temporal expression following damage or infection depends on the presence of specific mutations or environmental factors. Functional genomics aims at extracting an interpretation from large-scale sequencing data sets; i.e., inferring the functions of individual genes and how they interact with one another to influence disease states and cell activities. As opposed to traditional structural Genomics, which focuses on the DNA sequence of a genome, functional genomics emphasizes the protein composition of genomes and their roles in cellular processes. Furthermore, unlike genomics, which uses single-nucleotide polymorphism (SNPs) as a basic unit of analysis, functional genomics studies use variants associated with physiological traits or diseases.

Knowledge of gene functions can be used to develop new drugs, diagnose genetic disorders, and better understand cell physiology. For example, some genes are involved in regulating cell death and inflammation. Damaging a gene that is essential for these biological processes could lead to certain cancers like colon, breast, and prostate. The development of RNA molecules that target specific genes could open up new avenues for treating these diseases by providing a means to selectively silence the aberrant gene. Another example would be using zinc finger nukes (ZFNs) as a tool to induce apoptosis (programmed cell death) in cancer cells. ZFNs contain sequences that bind to specific chromosomes and upon binding induce apoptosis. Plasmatic exosomes carrying short hairpin RNAs targeting every chromosome in the human genome have also been utilized as a drug delivery system  to trigger programmed cell deaths in tumour cells.

Gaiman weaves together several story lines into a compelling picture of ancient witchcraft and its modern-day legacy. That said, the show did a good job of expanding upon the mythology of each character, giving them their own set of motivations and agendas. 

Overall, Gaiman provides a delicious double take on traditional fairy tales, where the goodies finally get shafted by the villains, but the story itself doesn't necessarily move forward towards a happy ending. prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task, paired with an input that provides further context. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
From the given text, list me details about parity plot

### Input:
A parity plot is a scatterplot that compares a set of results from a computational model against benchmark data. Each point has coordinates (x, y), where x is a benchmark value and y is the corresponding value from the model.

A line of the equation y = x, representing perfect model performance, is sometimes added as a reference. Where the model successfully reproduces a benchmark, that point will lie on the line.

Parity plots are found in scientific papers and reports, when the author wishes to validate a model in a visual way. However, when the data have a wide range, the large scale makes important discrepancies invisible and the model appears better than it actually is. In that case, a plot of model errors [(y-x) vs. x] is better for evaluating the performance of the model.

### Response:
generated_text:
1. Aparity plot is a scatterplot that shows the comparison of a set of results from a computational model against benchmark data.
2. The X-axis represents the benchmark values and the Y-axis represents the corresponding values from the model.
3. A line of the equation y = x, which represents perfect model performance, is often added as a reference.
4. When the model successfully reproduces a benchmark, that point will lie on the line.
5. Parity plots are used to compare the performance of different models in scientific papers and reports.
6. For evaluating the performance of the model, a plot of model errors ((Y-X) vs. X) is better.

### Instruction:
Given the following paragraph on cell structure, what is an example of membrane proteins?

### Input:
Every cell is enclosed within a cell membrane that separates its cytoplasm from the extracellular space.[41] A cell membrane consists of a lipid bilayer, including cholesterols that sit between phospholipids to maintain their fluidity at various temperatures. Cell membranes are semipermeable, allowing small molecules such as oxygen, carbon dioxide, and water to pass through while restricting the movement of larger molecules and charged particles such as ions.[42] Cell membranes also contains membrane proteins, including integral membrane proteins that go across the membrane serving as membrane transporters, and peripheral proteins that loosely attach to the outer side of the cell membrane, acting as enzymes shaping the cell.[43] Cell membranes are involved in various cellular processes such as cell adhesion, storing electrical energy, and cell signalling and serve as the attachment surface for several extracellular structures such as a cell wall, glycocalyx, and cytoskeleton.

### Response:
generated_text: Membrane proteins include both integrative (that interact with other membranes) and Peripheral proteins which act as enzymes shaped the cell by attaching to the outside of the membrane. An example of an integrative membrane protein is Orai, a channel protein found restrictedly on the inner surface of the mitochondrial matrix.

Languages spoken in the Republic of India belong to several language families, the major ones being the Indo-Aryan languages spoken by 78.05% of Indians and the Dravidian languages spoken by 19.64% of Indians, both families together are sometimes known as Indic languages.Languages spoken by the remaining 2.31% of the population belong to the Austroasiatic, Sino–Tibetan, Tai–Kadai, and a few other minor language families and isolates.  As per the People's Linguistic Survey of India, India has the second highest number of languages (780), after Papua New Guinea (840). Ethnologue lists a lower number of 456.

### Response:
generated_text:
The terms "Indic" or "Indican" can be used at various times to denote the set of languages common to the Indian subcontinent. Used most frequently in modern usage, these languages share a western affinity with languages from North America, particularly English, and a eastern affinity with languages from Asia, particularly Chinese. In certain senses, they may also be considered to share a northeastern affinity with Uyghur and Tartar sources. Etymology aside, Indic languages generally aim to capture essence rather than specific identity. This universality has led some—especially in the West—to view them as a single entity.[6] The related but separate languages of South east asia (such as Malay) are often kept out of discussion.

Although not all linguistic scholars agree, most agree that the term "Indica" or "Indican" accurately reflects the umbrella term for this multi-lingual continent. See Endangered languages.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
What is Occam's razor?

### Response:
generated_text:
Occam’s Razor is a philosophical axiom used in English reasoning and mathematics, asserting that any statement must apportion 100% of its credit to exactly one cause. More specifically, it states that whenever someone says “A Thing happened;” you should ask yourself: “must A have happened by itself?”, “Was there some external factor that caused it?” or similar questions. 

The philosophy behind Occam’s Razor is simple: human explanations are complicated, so we need to look at things individually to understand them. If something did not occur by itself, then likely there was a cause that led to it happening. If there is evidence for two causes being combined, then either both were true independently, or one caused the other to happen.

Round one is always the same as there are no trades in round one. The order of picks 1-7 are all considered equal for purposes of comparing draft range. After the first seven picks are made, any remaining picks are accounted for as part of the "surprise" draft. Surprises can occur anywhere through the last two rounds of the festival, and if a team has a very strong prediction for their own pick, they may choose to pass on the opportunity to select someone else's player.

What are the official forms of written Norwegian?

### Input:
As established by law and government policy, the two official forms of written Norwegian are Bokmål (literally 'book tongue') and Nynorsk ('new Norwegian'). The official Norwegian Language Council (Språkrådet) is responsible for regulating the two forms, and recommends the terms Norwegian Bokmål and Norwegian Nynorsk in English.[citation needed] Two other written forms without official status also exist. One, called Riksmål ('national language'), is today to a large extent the same language as Bokmål though somewhat closer to the Danish language. It is regulated by the unofficial Norwegian Academy, which translates the name as 'Standard Norwegian'. The other is Høgnorsk ('High Norwegian'), a more purist form of Nynorsk, which maintains the language in an original form as given by Ivar Aasen and rejects most of the reforms from the 20th century; this form has limited use.

### Instruction:
When and where was the first major agitation led by Gandhi, tell me who this agitation was against,  which crop led to this agitation and why this agitation happened.

### Input:
Gandhi's first major achievement came in 1917 with the Champaran agitation in Bihar. The Champaran agitation pitted the local peasantry against largely Anglo-Indian plantation owners who were backed by the local administration. How do you throw a cutter in baseball?

A cutter is considered to be one of the most dangerous tools a pitcher has.  Because it can be thrown with such different motion, any pitch mechanics not optimized for generating a cutter will result in a less effective cutter.  Some examples include fade away on move or late movement prayers.  

To throw a cutter, start by maintaining your normal striking position and taking steps to prepare to throw.  You'll want to look up high and forward in the mound, feet planted, and body ready to heave the ball upstairs.  Tap your index finger on the upper half of the ball before releasing it.  Look down at the baby carriage shape in front of you and visualize a sound traveling through the infield dirt.  This sound is what will eventually become a cutter when released from underneath your fingers.  With the sound as reference, look to create a motion similar to the look-down gesture while throwing.  Trust your gut and go with the flow until you feel like you are comfortable and the action feels natural.  Then apply repetition until the movement becomes second nature. 

Once you are comfortable, practice comparing your results against a couple other scenarios.  First, consider practicing your usual delivery without the cutter present.  Do you notice a change in velocity and accuracy?  Second, think about how you can vary your release point.  Try holding the ball at different levels above the ground to see if you notice any differences in path or travel distance.  Finally, consider practicing with a partner.  Observe their reaction and copy their motion if it looks good.  But most importantly, enjoy the process and have fun!

Within each category though, there is room to classify further. For example, rock music could have sub-genres like metal, alternative, electronica, etc. Hip hop music has sub-genres too like gangsta rap, female empowerment, and lo-fi. Electronic music has sub-genres dedicated to house, techno, trance, drum and bass, collaborative电音 (electronic dance music), etc.

And within those subgenres, there is further division. Example, Drum & Bass is one sub-genre of electronic music. It’s own dedicated DJ spins off several related sub-sets: Dubstep, Drum and Bass festivals, Production drones, etc.

The dividing line between genres becomes increasingly blurred as you ask for more and more levels of granularity. At this point, any genre or way of classifying music feels extremely subjective. Each person has their own preferences and ranking of genres based on how they listen to music. This is why music appreciation is so unique to each individual - we all hear and perceive music differently.

### Instruction:
What is Sinking Sand?

### Input:
Quicksand, also known as sinking sand, is a colloid consisting of fine granular material (such as sand, silt or clay) and water. It forms in saturated loose sand when the sand is suddenly agitated. When water in the sand cannot escape, it creates a liquefied soil that loses strength and cannot support weight. Quicksand can form in standing water or in upward flowing water (as from an artesian spring). In the case of upward flowing water, forces oppose the force of gravity and suspend the soil particles.

prompt:
Below is an instruction that describes a task. Write a response that appropriately completes the request.

### Instruction:
How do I train for a marathon?

### Response:
generated_text: A lot of people who run marathons will start training about six months before the race. You would typically start by running short lengths, such as 2 to 3 miles a day, 2 to 3 days per week after a couple weeks of that, you would build up to six or seven miles per day Often runners will run with a partner or in a group so they can encourage each other and push each other to go longer and harder on the occasional long run. About one month out is when you shored train the longest: Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Some runners will even take a break during the weekend and focus on their recovery. Once you are feeling well rested and ready to roll with the punches, you will want to begin your longest runs gradually increasing them until you hit about 10-12 miles per day. Most will suggest spending at least 4 hours running per week; however if you are dedicated, you can push yourself mentally to complete feats like running 30 minutes or more than 15 miles per day. When you are within about 6 months of your race, you should consider switching to shorter lapses in between runs. Within three weeks of your race, you should be able to run 10 miles without any pain. By the time you are five-six weeks out from your race, you should be comfortable running 7-8 miles. After six weeks, if you are still feeling sharp, you should consider doing tempo workouts with a pacer member along with some strength and core exercises. Through the last two weeks prior to your race, make sure you have pretty good mileage hit in both directions: uphill and downhill. For example, if you're racing in April, you'd target ~40 miles total together over 2 months. In the last week leading up to your race, rely on steady-state cardio (biking, jogging, etc) and no high-intensity exercise. The week before is when you'll taper down your activity level and really focus on getting enough sleep. On the race morning, make sure to get enough rest and eat a few snacks. Then around 1 hour before before you start, distribute water very strategically through out the course via cups or bottles. A couple hundred meters ahead of you are usually sufficient. Good luck!

What is mitosis

### Response:
generated_text:
Mitosis is a type of cell division that occurs only in multicellular organisms, such as mice and flies. It involves the condensation of chromosomes into a single nucleus and the splitting of that nucleus's contents, along with the formation of two genetically identical child cells from the combined DNA of the parent cell and its sibling chromatids. The process takes place inside the mitochondria first to preventDNA damage before moving to the nuclear membrane. Mitosis is anabolic, meaning it forms longs dynamically during the cell cycle, instead of desacribly at one point in time like huclear divisions. It is thus separate from, and often slower than, the exponential growth of, e.g., the growing of a tumor. The ease with which tumors can grow and develop depends on the capacity of the cellular machinery to repair damaged DNA on chromosome 10.

Most individuals undergomitosis approximately every 80 days, except for births, deaths, and inversions. One example of a genetic disorder due to chromosomal abnormalities is Down syndrome, associated with a deletion of the third common, non-homologous chromosome, most likely caused by male-to-female replication.

The contemporary national legal systems are generally based on one of four basic systems: civil law, common law, statutory law, religious law or combinations of these. However, the legal system of each country is shaped by its unique history and so incorporates individual variations.[1] The science that studies law at the level of legal systems is called comparative law.

Both civil (also known as Roman) and common law systems can be considered the most widespread in the world: civil law because it is the most widespread by landmass and by population overall, and common law because it is employed by the greatest number of people compared to any single civil law system.[2][3][4]

### Instruction:
Give me a list of the lines on the London Underground

### Response:
generated_text:
- The London Underground or the Tube is an underground passenger railway in London, England. It operates over the city's major roads and has dozens of routes operating on more than 200 miles of track.
- The exact number of lines varies from year to year as routes are added and removed. Currently there are 16 lines operating across three colors: blue, purple and black. Pink and Indian red lines add additional color options.
- All lines share a few common characteristics. They are all rapid rail systems with platform carriages, they all have driverless trains, and their coaches are painted in standardized colors.
- Most lines have two sets of windows for ventilation: open-facing window seats (called»open floor« seats) closest to the exterior of the subway car, and shielded-window seats further inside the coach. Windows on newer equipment may also have screens to prevent rain and snow interception while train driving. Alighting passengers are promptly served by station attendants who ensure that sufficient space is available before the next train arrives. When necessary, tracks can be divested of cars using electric traction vehicles; these are rarely used today but were needed during construction.
- Modernizations continue beyond maintenance of the physical lines. Subsequently built structures support extra-long subway lines (more than 400 feet long), allowing for greater capacity and efficiency. The longer lines enable cities to fill gaps in their public transportation networks, increasing independence for riders, and new services such as line priority for specific destinations. Future plans include building "slight improvements" to existing lines to increase capacity, extend hours, and attract more users.

Lightwater is a village in the Surrey Heath district of Surrey, England, about 27 miles (43 km) southwest of central London. It is bounded to the north-west by the M3 motorway and to the north-east by the "Lightwater Bypass" (A322). To the south-east it is bounded by the "Red Road" (B311), while to the south-west there are vehicle-testing tracks owned by the Ministry of Defence.

The village is adjacent to junction 3 of the M3 motorway. The nearest railway station is at Bagshot, on the line between Ascot and Aldershot. Lightwater is part of the civil parish of Windlesham, which has a population of 17,000 and also includes the neighbouring village of Bagshot.[2]
